facts.text: Miami was named after the Miami River, derived from Mayaimi, the historic name of Lake Okeechobee and the Native Americans who lived around it.
Julia Tuttle, dubbed “the mother of Miami,” convinced Henry Flagler to extend the Florida East Coast Railway to the area, sparking the city’s founding.
PortMiami is the world’s busiest cruise port, handling more than 1.5 million passengers annually.
The Rickenbacker Causeway, the southernmost causeway, connects Brickell to Virginia Key and Key Biscayne, offering sweeping ocean views.
Miami’s frequent portrayal in music, film, and popular culture has made its landmarks recognizable worldwide, from “Miami Vice” to “The Birdcage.”
The Vizcaya Museum and Gardens, a National Historic Landmark, sits on a 28‑acre estate in Coconut Grove and showcases early 20th‑century architecture.
